justification: Both terminology mismatches identified (session/token and suspended/locked). terminology_alignment section explicitly maps both pairs with clear definitions and origin notes. Brief uses consistent terminology throughout with guidance on when to use domain vs technical terms. Both ACs preserved with exact error codes SESSION_EXPIRED and ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED. Architecture details include Redis TTL 1800, last_accessed_at field, and token issuance gate for suspended accounts.

observations.text: terminology_alignment section is exceptionally well-structured — maps both pairs with domain_term/technical_term/definition and clear guidance on where each term should appear
contradictions_resolved section explicitly calls out the suspended/locked drift and resolves it with Bird as Domain Authority
Both error codes SESSION_EXPIRED and ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED preserved exactly in ACs and architecture guidance
Open questions identify genuine gaps (mid-session suspension, boundary condition) that show synthesis depth
Task context upfront tells Shaq exactly how to use the two vocabularies: Bird's terms in responses, MJ's terms in internals
Architecture guidance includes all three required details: Redis TTL 1800, last_accessed_at field, and token issuance step for locked accounts
